title: Pathogenesis and Treatment of Infectious Diseases (Faculty of Science)
convener: Doctor MR Abrahams (Division of Medical Virology) and Doctor C Moodley (Division of Medical Microbiology)
outline: This course provides the conceptual basis for understanding pathogenic bacteria, fungi, viruses and parasites, and particularly addresses the fundamental mechanisms of how they cause disease in humans. Topics include: how successful pathogens invade the host and escape immune defences; parts of the immune system important in resisting infection; mechanisms by which pathogens evade immune responses; antimicrobial therapy for bacterial, viral, fungal and parasitic infections; knowledge of pathogen life cycles to identify treatment targets; antimicrobial resistance mechanisms and novel approaches to combat them; and the role of antimicrobial stewardship. Through blended learning, practical sessions, field visits and interaction with experts in public health and infectious diseases, students are introduced to a variety of different career avenues.
lecture_times: Refer to departmental notices. Includes practical sessions, field trips and tutorials.
dp_requirements: 40% average in class tests or quizzes; and an average of 50% for assignments. Attendance at all practicals, field trips and tutorials. Submission of at least 80% of assignments.
assessment: Class record 60% (class tests and/or quizzes 20%, practical reports and tutorials 20%, assignments 20%); one 3-hour final examination written in June 40%. Supplementary examinations may be offered to students with an overall score of 45–49%.
